summary: High‑quality soundboard capture of the final night of Oakland ’95, featuring a standout Estimated Prophet and an explosive second set with David Murray.
review: The recording is a clean soundboard mix that lets Vince’s piano, the tight rhythm section and especially the drums shine. Musically the show is solid throughout, with a rare opening Let the Good Times Roll and a wildly hot second half after saxophonist David Murray joins, culminating in an unforgettable NFA/Estimated Prophet stretch.

ai_rating.rationale: Both reviewers praise the ensemble playing and recording quality; one gives a perfect 4‑star sound rating while the other rates the show overall at 3 stars, yielding an overall strong but not flawless score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear and balanced mix with straight piano tone, tight rhythm section, and drums that sound better than ever; no major drop‑outs or muffling reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A brief, tight first set with solid ensemble work and a rare opening Let the Good Times Roll; the second set starts a bit combobulated but gains momentum after David Murray’s sax entrance, building to an incendiary NFA/Estimated Prophet climax.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s playing is noted as sounding better than in Winter ’94, especially on the later jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir adds a unique acoustic‑guitar three‑song sequence that feels like an impromptu acoustic set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s straight piano sound is highlighted as giving the band a classy, full texture.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drums are described as sounding better than ever on this recording.
